response.justification: Bogdan has genuine geospatial domain exposure through GISCollective and Green Map, and demonstrates distributed systems thinking from his Dunnhumby work, but the role demands modern C++ or Rust as a primary language and the candidate's C++ experience is limited to roughly six months at National Instruments in 2014-2015 plus a thesis project. His entire professional career post-2015 is built on TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang, none of which are relevant to the core technical stack. There is also no evidence of routing tile pipeline work, navigation guidance systems, or the kind of latency-sensitive geospatial data transformation the role requires.
strengths: Geospatial domain familiarity via GISCollective open-source geo-data platform and Green Map GIS application
Hands-on distributed systems and high-throughput pipeline work at Dunnhumby (millions of HTTP requests/second, async I/O, low-latency data)
Broad systems exposure including Linux, Kubernetes, and some C++ components at National Instruments
concerns: C++ experience is minimal (~6 months in 2014) and no Rust experience, while both are primary requirements for the role
GISCollective is a data-collection platform, not a routing or navigation tile pipeline — geospatial depth does not map to the target domain
Currently in a career gap; most recent substantive engineering role was 6-month AI tooling work, not systems-level geospatial development
